name: University Of Florida/Florida Cooperative Extension - Citrus County Cooperative Extension Service
description_short: University of Florida's Citrus County Extension offers free expert guidance on agriculture, financial planning, gardening, and youth development through Master Money Mentors and trained agents.
description_long:
University of Florida/IFAS Citrus County Cooperative Extension Service is a public education and outreach program operated by the University of Florida's Institute of Food and Agricultural Sciences. It functions as a free resource center providing expert consultation on practical life issues affecting residents of Citrus County, Florida. The program is part of a broader cooperative extension network that bridges university research and community needs.

The organization offers multiple pathways for consumer assistance. Master Money Mentors specifically help residents navigate complex financial issues at no cost. Agriculture and Natural Resources Agents provide farming expertise, while Master Gardeners offer horticultural guidance. The Extension also covers work/life balance issues, youth development through 4-H programming, and natural resource conservation. All services are available through phone consultation, in-person visits at their Lecanto office, and educational programming throughout the county.

What distinguishes this service is its direct affiliation with a major university research institution, meaning guidance is grounded in evidence-based practices rather than sales-driven recommendations. As a public extension service, there are no commercial interests or profit motives—the organization exists to serve the community. Services are entirely free, and the Master Money Mentors program specifically targets financial planning without selling products or services.

The main caveat is that Extension Services, while high-quality and trustworthy, are generalist resources rather than specialized financial counseling. Master Money Mentors help with "complicated financial issues" but the website does not detail specific expertise in credit repair, debt settlement, bankruptcy, or other specialized financial recovery services. This is best suited for general financial guidance and planning rather than crisis intervention.
pros: Master Money Mentors program provides free financial guidance for complicated financial issues
No commercial interests or product sales—purely educational public service
Staffed by university-trained experts with access to UF research and evidence-based practices
Multiple service areas beyond finance (agriculture, gardening, youth development, natural resources)
Free services with no cost to residents
Local physical office in Lecanto with set business hours for in-person consultation
Affiliated with major research institution (University of Florida/IFAS)
cons: Website provides limited detail about specific financial services and Master Money Mentor expertise
Not a specialized credit counseling or financial crisis intervention service
Limited hours (Monday-Friday, 8am-5pm only) may not accommodate all schedules
Generalist resource rather than specialized for credit repair, debt relief, or bankruptcy guidance
No indication of HUD-certification or NFCC-certification status on the website
best_for: Florida residents seeking general financial planning and money management guidance
Agricultural professionals and small farmers needing expert cultivation and resource advice
Homeowners and gardeners wanting landscape and plant disease management support
Families interested in youth development programs and 4-H participation
diagnosis: Best for Citrus County residents seeking free, university-backed general financial guidance and planning rather than specialized credit or debt services. Primary caveat: this is a generalist Extension Service, not a specialized financial counseling organization, so those needing credit repair, debt settlement, or bankruptcy guidance should seek services from NFCC-certified credit counselors or specialized providers.
services: Master Gardener landscape and pollinator consulting
Plant disease management and prevention education
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3650 W. Sovereign Path, Ste. 1, Lecanto, FL 34461
